2604.08870.title: Temporal Dropout Risk in Learning Analytics: A Harmonized Survival Benchmark Across Dynamic and Early-Window Representations
2604.08870.paper_summary: Student dropout is a persistent concern in Learning Analytics, yet comparative studies frequently evaluate predictive models under heterogeneous protocols, prioritizing discrimination over temporal interpretability and calibration. This study introduces a survival-oriented benchmark for temporal dropout risk modelling using the Open University Learning Analytics Dataset (OULAD). Two harmonized arms are compared: a dynamic weekly arm, with models in person-period representation, and a comparable continuous-time arm, with an expanded roster of families -- tree-based survival, parametric, and neural models. The evaluation protocol integrates four analytical layers: predictive performance, ablation, explainability, and calibration. Results are reported within each arm separately, as a single cross-arm ranking is not methodologically warranted. Within the comparable arm, Random Survival Forest leads in discrimination and horizon-specific Brier scores; within the dynamic arm, Poisson Piecewise-Exponential leads narrowly on integrated Brier score within a tight five-family cluster. No-refit bootstrap sampling variability qualifies these positions as directional signals rather than absolute superiority. Ablation and explainability analyses converged, across all families, on a shared finding: the dominant predictive signal was not primarily demographic or structural, but temporal and behavioral. Calibration corroborated this pattern in the better-discriminating models, with the exception of XGBoost AFT, which exhibited systematic bias. These results support the value of a harmonized, multi-dimensional benchmark in Learning Analytics and situate dropout risk as a temporal-behavioral process rather than a function of static background attributes.
2604.08870.paper_summary_zh: 學生輟學是學習分析中持續關注的問題，但比較研究經常在異質性協議下評估預測模型，優先考慮區分能力而非時間可解釋性和校準。這項研究引入了一個以生存為導向的基準，用於使用開放大學學習分析數據集（OULAD）進行時間輟學風險建模。比較了兩個協調的臂：一個動態每週臂，模型以人-期表示，另一個可比較的連續時間臂，擁有擴展的模型家族——基於樹的生存模型、參數模型和神經模型。評估協議整合了四個分析層面：預測性能、消融、可解釋性和校準。結果在每個臂內分別報告，因為單一的跨臂排名在方法論上並不合理。在可比較的臂內，隨機生存森林在區分能力和特定時間範圍的布里爾分數中領先；在動態臂內，泊松分段指數模型在緊密的五個模型家族中，整體布里爾分數略微領先。無重擬引導抽樣變異性使這些位置被視為方向性信號，而非絕對優越性。消融和可解釋性分析在所有模型家族中收斂到一個共同的發現：主導的預測信號並非主要來自人口統計或結構性因素，而是時間和行為性因素。校準支持了這一模式在更具區分性的模型中，除了XGBoost AFT，該模型顯示出系統性偏差。這些結果支持了在學習分析中建立協調的多維基準的價值，並將輟學風險視為一個時間-行為過程，而非靜態背景屬性的函數。
2604.08870.author: Rafael da Silva et.al.
2604.08870.authors: Rafael da Silva, Jeff Eicher, Gregory Longo
